user_message: Just got this from David Rivera at Fourth Coffee -- I need help figuring out migration architecture. Not urgent but would be good to have by EOD.

input_artifacts.content:
From: David Rivera <david.rivera@fourthcoffee.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-13 12:56 EST
Subject: Follow-up: migration architecture Review

Our team encountered some challenges with migration architecture. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: David Rivera <david.rivera@fourthcoffee.com>
Date: 2024-02-26 11:15 EST
Subject: migration architecture - Action Required

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with migration architecture. What are the next steps from your side?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF40-A trap (Over-reads commitment (treats general enthusiasm as scope)); produces complete Commitment assessment, BPF advance verdict, milestone proposal, forecast category recommendation, ac
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF40-A failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF40-A failure mode (Over-reads commitment (treats general enthusiasm as scope))
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct migration architecture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF40-A -- Over-reads commitment (treats general enthusiasm as scope)
